title: malus sylvestris var. eleyi
intro: Malus sylvestris var. eleyi, also known as the wild apple or Eley's crab apple, is a variety of the wild apple tree native to the Caucasus region. It is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly in traditional medicine, where it is used to treat digestive issues, fever, and skin conditions due to its astringent and anti-inflammatory properties. The health benefits of Malus sylvestris var. eleyi include reducing inflammation, improving digestion, and supporting the immune system. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into teas, tinctures, and infusions, and are often used in combination with other herbs to enhance their effects.
